name: Rainbow Body
description: The Rainbow Body (Tibetan: འཇའ་ལུས་, Wylie: 'ja' lus; Sanskrit: indradhanu kaya) is a concept primarily associated with Tibetan Buddhism, particularly Dzogchen, and some traditions of Bön. It describes the purported transformation of a realized practitioner's physical body at the time of death into a body of pure light or energy. Proponents claim that through intense spiritual practice and purification, the physical body can dissolve, leaving behind only hair, nails, and clothing. In some accounts, the body shrinks significantly or vanishes entirely. The phenomenon is seen as evidence of complete enlightenment and liberation from the cycle of rebirth. Claims of rainbow body transformation have been documented throughout Tibetan Buddhist history, with proponents pointing to specific individuals and circumstances as evidence. Mainstream Buddhist scholars generally view these claims with skepticism, often interpreting them metaphorically or psychologically. Some suggest that reported cases may be due to natural decomposition processes, misunderstandings, or exaggerations of unusual circumstances surrounding death. While acknowledging the profound spiritual aspirations and practices underlying the concept, mainstream scholars typically do not accept the literal interpretation of physical transformation into light. The Rainbow Body is considered one of the highest attainments in Dzogchen practice, signifying the complete realization of emptiness and the union of wisdom and compassion. It is often linked to the concept of the Sambhogakaya, the enjoyment body of a Buddha.
subtitle: Transformation into Light at Death

claims.claim: Physical body transforms into light at death.
claims.evidence: Reports of bodies shrinking, disappearing, or only leaving behind hair/nails.
claims.criticism: Skeptical interpretations focus on natural decomposition, misinterpretations, and metaphorical understanding of enlightenment.

claims.claim: Attainment of Rainbow Body signifies complete enlightenment.
claims.evidence: Associated with advanced spiritual practice and realization of emptiness.
claims.criticism: Enlightenment is primarily a mental and spiritual state, not necessarily a physical alteration.

claims.claim: Rainbow Body transformation is a historical and ongoing phenomenon.
claims.evidence: Accounts of specific individuals achieving Rainbow Body throughout Tibetan Buddhist history.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable scientific evidence and reliance on anecdotal accounts makes objective validation difficult.

keyFigures.name: Padmasambhava
keyFigures.role: Considered by many to be the founder of Tibetan Buddhism and a key figure in Dzogchen teachings.

keyFigures.name: Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche
keyFigures.role: A highly respected Dzogchen master whose death was reportedly accompanied by rainbow phenomena.

keyFigures.name: Georges Dreyfus
keyFigures.role: Scholar who critically examines claims of extraordinary abilities in Tibetan Buddhism.

timeline.event: Padmasambhava introduces Dzogchen teachings to Tibet.
Reported instances of Rainbow Body transformations throughout Tibetan history.
Continued discussions and debates surrounding the Rainbow Body phenomenon.

sources.author: Georges Dreyfus
sources.work: The Sound of Two Hands Clapping: The Education of a Tibetan Buddhist Monk
sources.title: Georges Dreyfus

sources.author: Lopez, Donald S. Jr.
sources.work: Prisoners of Shangri-La: Tibetan Buddhism and the West
sources.title: Lopez, Donald S. Jr.
